overview.tagline: A diverse, affordable borough on the Hackensack River with strong community pride
overview.description: Little Ferry is a small, diverse borough located on the banks of the Hackensack River in southern Bergen County. Known for its affordability, community spirit, and convenient location, Little Ferry offers an accessible entry point to Bergen County living. The borough's working-class roots and multicultural population create a welcoming, unpretentious atmosphere. With easy access to Routes 46 and 17, the NJ Turnpike, and NJ Transit bus service, Little Ferry provides solid transportation options for commuters while maintaining a strong neighborhood identity.
highlights: One of the most affordable communities in Bergen County
Diverse, multicultural population with strong community bonds
Hackensack River waterfront access and parks
Convenient highway access to Routes 46, 17, and the NJ Turnpike
Close to American Dream, MetLife Stadium, and Meadowlands attractions

realEstate.assessmentInfo: Properties are assessed by the Bergen County Tax Assessor's office. Little Ferry offers some of the most affordable housing in Bergen County.
realEstate.recentTrends: Little Ferry has seen increasing interest from buyers priced out of more expensive Bergen County towns. The borough's affordability and transit access have driven steady appreciation. The mix of single-family homes, two-family properties, and condominiums provides options for various budgets.
neighborhoodNotes: Liberty Street area — Near the Hackensack River with waterfront views
Washington Avenue — Main commercial corridor with shops and services
Main Street — Residential area with a mix of housing types
Northern section — Quieter residential streets near Ridgefield Park border

trashCollection.title: Trash Collection
trashCollection.description: Curbside trash pickup is provided twice weekly by the Borough of Little Ferry.
details: Pickup occurs twice per week on designated days
Trash must be placed curbside by 6:00 AM on collection day
Use approved containers with secure lids
Bulk item pickup available by appointment
Holiday schedule adjustments posted on borough website

recycling.title: Recycling
recycling.description: Curbside recycling is collected weekly by the Borough.
details: Commingled recyclables collected weekly
Paper and cardboard collected on the same schedule
Recycling bins available through the DPW
Yard waste collected seasonally
Electronics recycling events held periodically

waterSewer.title: Water & Sewer
waterSewer.description: Water and sewer service is managed by the Borough of Little Ferry.
details: Water and sewer billing handled by the Borough
Water supplied through the borough water system
Sewer service maintained by the DPW
Water quality reports published annually
Emergency repairs available through the DPW

permits.title: Building & Permits
permits.description: The Building Department handles construction permits, zoning, and code enforcement.
details: Building permits required for construction and renovations
Zoning applications reviewed by the Planning Board and Zoning Board
Permit applications available at Borough Hall
Inspections scheduled through the Building Department
Flood zone permits required for properties in FEMA-designated areas

parking.title: Parking
parking.description: The Borough manages on-street parking and municipal lots.
details: On-street parking available on most residential streets
Municipal parking available near downtown
Overnight parking restrictions on certain streets
Snow emergency parking bans enforced
No metered parking in the borough

additionalServices.title: Little Ferry Public Library
additionalServices.description: Public library serving borough residents with books, programs, and digital resources.
details: Located at 239 Liberty Street
Children's programs and summer reading
Part of the Bergen County Cooperative Library System (BCCLS)
Free Wi-Fi and computer access

additionalServices.title: Recreation Department
details: Youth sports leagues and summer programs
Senior citizen programs and events
Community events throughout the year
Borough parks and athletic facilities

noise.title: Noise Ordinance
noise.summary: Little Ferry enforces noise regulations to protect residential quality of life.
keyRules: Construction noise prohibited before 7:00 AM and after 8:00 PM on weekdays
Saturday construction allowed 8:00 AM to 6:00 PM
No construction noise on Sundays or holidays
Power equipment restricted to daytime hours
Excessive noise subject to police enforcement

propertyMaintenance.title: Property Maintenance
propertyMaintenance.summary: Property owners must maintain their properties in good condition.
keyRules: Lawns must be maintained at a reasonable height
Sidewalk snow removal required within 24 hours
Properties must be kept free of debris
Abandoned vehicles must be removed
Fences must comply with borough requirements

pets.title: Pet Regulations
pets.summary: Little Ferry requires dog licensing and enforces leash laws.
keyRules: All dogs over 7 months must be licensed annually
Dogs must be leashed on all public property
Pet waste must be picked up immediately
Nuisance animals subject to enforcement
No exotic or wild animals permitted

parking.title: Parking Regulations
parking.summary: Street parking is regulated throughout the borough.
keyRules: Overnight parking restricted on certain borough streets
Snow emergency parking bans enforced
Commercial vehicles restricted on residential streets
Parking on sidewalks prohibited
Handicapped parking violations subject to fines

rentals.title: Rental Property Regulations
rentals.summary: Rental properties must comply with borough housing codes.
keyRules: Rental properties must be registered with the Borough
Certificate of Habitability required
Landlords must maintain properties to code
Inspections at change of tenancy
Multi-family dwellings subject to additional fire codes

other.title: Flood Zone Regulations
other.summary: Little Ferry has areas in FEMA-designated flood zones, requiring special building and insurance requirements.
keyRules: Properties in flood zones require flood insurance
Construction in flood zones subject to elevated building requirements
Flood damage prevention ordinance applies to all development
Borough participates in the NFIP Community Rating System
other.source: Borough of Little Ferry Flood Damage Prevention Ordinance

publicSchools.name: Little Ferry Public School No. 1 (Washington School)
publicSchools.notes: Elementary school serving the borough's youngest students

publicSchools.name: Little Ferry Public School No. 2 (Memorial School)
publicSchools.notes: Middle school serving grades 5-8 with comprehensive programming

nearbyHigherEd: New Jersey Institute of Technology (Newark) — 25 minutes

whyMoveHere: One of the most affordable communities in Bergen County
Diverse, multicultural population with a welcoming atmosphere
Convenient highway access to Routes 46, 17, and the NJ Turnpike
Close to Meadowlands entertainment (MetLife, American Dream)
NJ Transit bus service to NYC

commuteToNYC.duration: 35-55 minutes to Port Authority Bus Terminal
commuteToNYC.notes: NJ Transit bus routes provide service to the Port Authority Bus Terminal in Manhattan. Driving to Secaucus Junction for NJ Transit rail is another option. Routes 46 and 17 provide highway access.

costOfLiving.summary: Little Ferry is one of the most affordable places to live in Bergen County, with housing prices well below the county median and a lower barrier to entry for first-time homebuyers.
costOfLiving.vsStateAverage: Approximately 5% above the New Jersey state average

pros: Very affordable by Bergen County standards
Close to major entertainment and shopping in the Meadowlands
Waterfront access on the Hackensack River
cons: Some areas in FEMA flood zones — flood insurance required
No direct train service — bus commute to NYC
School ratings below Bergen County averages
Industrial areas along the river and highway corridors
Borough was significantly impacted by Hurricane Sandy in 2012

restaurants.name: La Forchetta
restaurants.description: Italian restaurant offering homemade pasta, classic entrees, and a warm family atmosphere.

restaurants.name: Manila's Filipino Cuisine
restaurants.description: Authentic Filipino restaurant serving traditional dishes like adobo, sinigang, and lumpia, reflecting the borough's diverse community.

restaurants.name: Little Ferry Diner
restaurants.description: Classic neighborhood diner serving breakfast, lunch, and dinner with American and Greek specialties.

restaurants.name: Tops Diner Express
restaurants.description: Casual eatery offering quick-service diner fare, sandwiches, and daily specials.

parks.name: Borough Park
parks.description: Main borough park with playgrounds, athletic fields, basketball courts, and picnic areas.

parks.name: Lakeview Field
parks.description: Athletic fields and recreational area used for borough sports leagues and community events.

parks.name: Hackensack River Waterfront
parks.description: Waterfront area along the Hackensack River offering scenic views and walking opportunities.

events.name: Little Ferry Community Day
events.description: Annual community celebration with food vendors, games, music, and activities for families.
events.when: Summer, typically in June or July

events.name: Memorial Day Ceremony
events.description: Annual ceremony honoring veterans with a parade and memorial service.

events.name: Holiday Tree Lighting
events.description: Annual holiday tree lighting with music, refreshments, and community gathering.

attractions.name: American Dream
attractions.description: Massive entertainment and shopping complex in nearby East Rutherford featuring a water park, theme park, ski slope, and hundreds of retail stores.
attractions.address: 1 American Dream Way, East Rutherford, NJ 07073

attractions.name: MetLife Stadium
attractions.description: Home of the NFL's New York Giants and New York Jets, located in the nearby Meadowlands complex.
attractions.address: 1 MetLife Stadium Drive, East Rutherford, NJ 07073

attractions.name: Hackensack River Greenway
attractions.description: Developing greenway along the Hackensack River providing walking and cycling opportunities.

shoppingAreas: Washington Avenue — Local shops and services
Route 46 corridor — Major retail and dining nearby

government.meetingSchedule: Borough Council meets the 2nd and 4th Tuesday of each month at 7:00 PM
government.meetingLocation: Borough Hall, 215-217 Liberty Street, Little Ferry, NJ 07643

utilities.provider: Borough of Little Ferry Water Department
